title: NOC 22100 – Chemical Technologists and Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: Chemical Technologists and Technicians working in Canada are in steady demand across manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, petrochemicals, utilities, government labs, and environmental-testing facilities. This occupation is classified under TEER 2, making it well-positioned for:

classification.teer: 2 – College diploma / apprenticeship or supervisory roles

duties: Conduct chromatography, spectroscopy, and chemical analyses
Operate pilot plants and lab equipment
employment_requirements: 1–3-year college diploma in chemical / biochemical technology
Eligibility through the Canadian Society for Chemical Technology
Licensing in certain provinces such as Quebec and Alberta
additional_information: Demand has risen strongly since the pandemic, particularly in:
Pharma & biotech labsPetrochemical processingFood manufacturingEnvironmental monitoringR&D pilot plants
Annual earnings typically range from $34,200–$83,200 CAD, depending on province, specialization, and certification.

High recruitment across industrial provincesPR-eligible technical occupationStable lab & industrial rolesPublic healthcare accessWork-life balance in regulated environmentsOpportunities to upgrade into engineering or supervisory careers
